title: hamiltonia inodora
intro: Hamiltonia inodora, also known as Sweet Viburnum or Wild Raisin, is a flowering plant native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its clusters of small white or pink flowers and edible fruit. The plant's leaves and berries are used in traditional medicine, rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which provide medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Hamiltonia inodora include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and berries can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are also used in skincare products to promote healthy skin and reduce the appearance of wrinkles.
